stories.headline: OpenAI Proposes Handing the U.S. Government a 5% Stake Worth $42.6 Billion
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI has proposed donating a 5% equity stake worth about $42.6 billion to a U.S. government sovereign wealth fund modeled on Alaska's Permanent Fund, a conceptual, unsigned pitch that would likely require congressional approval.

pull_quote.text: The Public Should Own Half of the Big A.I. Companies

stories.article_excerpt: In the span of a single week, OpenAI watched Washington delay its flagship GPT-5.6 launch, then floated a 5% stake for the government.

stories.headline: Venice AI Becomes a Unicorn With a $65 Million Series A for Private, Uncensored AI
stories.key_takeaway: Venice AI raised a $65M Series A at a $1B valuation, becoming a profitable unicorn by betting that private, uncensored AI is a real market and a rebuke to the industry's data-retention consensus.

pull_quote.text: We're optimizing for freedom and actually respecting users as adults, which is, I think, rare these days.

stories.article_excerpt: Two years after launching against the AI industry's prevailing instincts, Venice AI has become a profitable unicorn.

stories.headline: TwelveLabs Raises $100 Million to Build Video Superintelligence
stories.key_takeaway: TwelveLabs raised a $100M Series B co-led by NEA and NAVER Ventures to push beyond video foundation models into a full-stack 'video superintelligence' system, bringing total funding past $207M.

pull_quote.text: Five years ago, we made a contrarian bet: the substrate of machine intelligence is recorded reality in motion, not language. The road to Video Superintelligence starts here.

data_points.label: Share of world's data that is video

stories.article_excerpt: Most of the world's data is trapped inside video, and almost none of it can be searched. TwelveLabs wants to change that.

stories.headline: Nvidia Offers Startups Compute in Exchange for Revenue Share
stories.key_takeaway: Nvidia is offering fast-growing AI startups GPU access and token credits in exchange for a share of their future revenue, deepening its lock-in while raising fresh circular-financing concerns.

pull_quote.text: AI-native companies need access to scalable, energy- and cost-efficient compute infrastructure to compete globally.

stories.article_excerpt: Nvidia has spent the AI boom selling shovels. Now it wants a cut of the gold, offering compute for a slice of revenue.

stories.headline: OpenAI Launches a Partner Network to Scale Enterprise Deployments
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI launched a global Partner Network of consultancies and systems integrators to certify hundreds of thousands of consultants and win the enterprise AI deployment race against Anthropic and Microsoft.

pull_quote.text: Our collaboration with OpenAI establishes an operating model to unlock AI value at scale, uniting technology, talent, and transformation playbooks so clients can move decisively from pilots to performance.

stories.article_excerpt: OpenAI has decided the hardest part of enterprise AI is no longer building the model. It is getting the model deployed.

stories.headline: Blackstone Commits $30 Billion to AI Data Centers in Japan
stories.key_takeaway: Blackstone plans to invest about $30 billion in AI data centers in Japan over three to five years, betting that a global compute shortage, not an AI bubble, is the real risk.

pull_quote.text: There's a lot of concern that there's going to be a bubble in compute building. I actually think the risk is there's a shortage.

stories.article_excerpt: Blackstone is preparing to plant one of the largest private-capital flags yet in Asia's artificial-intelligence buildout.

stories.headline: Unisound's U2 Is a 266-Billion-Parameter MoE Model Built for Agents
stories.key_takeaway: Unisound's U2 is a sparse 266B/10B-active MoE model priced and engineered for cost-efficient, multi-step agentic execution, marking another Chinese entry in the 2026 race to build cheap, work-completing AI.
stories.article_excerpt: Chinese AI firm Unisound has released U2, a 266-billion-parameter MoE model that activates just 10 billion parameters per token.

stories.headline: Claude Enterprise Adds Admin Analytics, Model Entitlements, and Spend Alerts
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic added per-group cost analytics, model-level entitlements, and spend-threshold alerts to Claude Enterprise on July 2, 2026, giving admins and finance teams the guardrails to govern agentic AI spend as adoption scales.

pull_quote.text: Cost visibility isn't a once-a-month exercise. Granular spend data and alerts give teams regular nudges to reassess how they're using Claude, instead of a surprise at the end of the billing cycle.

stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic wants the people who sign the checks for Claude to stop being surprised by them.

stories.headline: A High Schooler's AI Uncovers 1.5 Million Previously Invisible Cosmic Objects
stories.key_takeaway: Pasadena high schooler Matteo Paz built an AI model called VARnet that mined NASA's NEOWISE archive to identify roughly 1.5 million previously uncataloged cosmic objects, earning a single-author Astronomical Journal paper.

pull_quote.text: The model I implemented can be used for other time domain studies in astronomy, and potentially anything else that comes in a temporal format.
pull_quote.role: Student researcher, Caltech/IPAC; creator of VARnet

stories.article_excerpt: When a NASA telescope stares at the whole sky for a decade, it captures far more than it was built to count.

stories.headline: D-I-TASSER Fuses AI With Physics to Crack Complex Protein Structures
stories.key_takeaway: D-I-TASSER, a hybrid AI-plus-physics pipeline from NUS's Zhang Lab, reports beating AlphaFold2 and AlphaFold3 on complex multi-domain proteins by roughly 13%, showing that grounding deep learning in physical simulation still wins on the hardest targets.

pull_quote.text: A protein's shape determines what it does in the body, but many large, multi-domain proteins are too complex for existing tools to model reliably.

stories.article_excerpt: For five years the story of computational biology has been AlphaFold. A hybrid model now challenges it on the hardest proteins.

stories.headline: White House Races to Finalize Voluntary Standards for Frontier AI Releases
stories.key_takeaway: The White House is finishing voluntary standards with OpenAI, Google and Anthropic that would give the government a pre-release review role over frontier AI models, with an announcement possibly the week of July 7.

pull_quote.text: We've made clear to the U.S. government that this is not our preferred long term model, and will work with them and others in industry to achieve a more sustainable approach for future releases.

stories.article_excerpt: The White House is racing to finalize voluntary standards governing how the most powerful AI systems reach the public.
